task_completion.description: The name 'Tokyo' is entered for the ground station.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the GPredict 'Add Ground Station' or 'Edit Ground Station' dialog, the 'Name' text field contains 'Tokyo'.
In the ground station dialog, the 'Location' text field contains 'Tokyo, Japan'.
In the ground station dialog, the 'Latitude' field contains '35.6762' and the direction is set to 'N' (North) or the value is positive.
In the ground station dialog, the 'Longitude' field contains '139.6503' and the direction is set to 'E' (East) or the value is positive.

task_completion.description: The altitude is set to 40 meters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the ground station dialog, the 'Altitude' field contains '40'.

task_completion.description: The ground station 'Tokyo' is successfully added to GPredict.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Tokyo' ground station appears in the list of available ground stations in GPredict's preferences or main interface, indicating the dialog was saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the GPredict GUI to add the ground station.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GPredict application windows and dialogs being interacted with, rather than terminal commands modifying configuration files.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GPredict application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches GPredict's standard UI, not a text editor or a mock-up.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected output into a text file to simulate completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The parameters are entered into the correct input fields within the GPredict application dialogs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task is a fully synthetic GUI interaction exercise requiring the user to add a new ground station named 'Tokyo' to GPredict. The local verifier is a stub, so evaluation relies entirely on visual confirmation of the correct parameters (Name: Tokyo, Location: Tokyo, Japan, Lat: 35.6762 N, Lon: 139.6503 E, Alt: 40) entered and saved in the GPredict GUI.
